results.title: Top 10 Box Alternatives in 2026 (Free & Paid) — Peony
results.content:
## Why Do Teams Look for Box Alternatives in 2026?

Box has built a reputation as the enterprise content management standard. It serves over 100,000 businesses, holds SOC 1/2/3, HIPAA, and FedRAMP certifications, and integrates deeply with both Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace. For large, regulated organizations, Box is a proven choice.

But not every team needs — or wants to pay for — an enterprise content governance platform. Purpose-built alternatives like Peony now offer page-level analytics, AI-powered organization, dynamic watermarking, and e-signatures at a fraction of Box's cost — with a free tier to start. Here are the most common reasons teams explore alternatives: [...] > TL;DR: Box is a powerful enterprise content platform used by 100,000+ businesses, but its per-user pricing ($15–$35/user/month), enterprise complexity, and limited external-sharing analytics push many teams toward alternatives. Peony (free) leads as the best Box alternative for external sharing — offering AI-powered data rooms, page-level analytics, and e-signatures with a free tier, plus dynamic watermarking on the Data Room plan ($52/admin/month). This guide compares 10 options across pricing, security, storage, and use case — updated for March 2026.

Last updated: July 2026

## Quick Comparison: Top 10 Box Alternatives (Ranked) [...] ## Frequently Asked Questions

### I run IT for a mid-size law firm and Box only shows that a file was opened — what's the best Box alternative for tracking how clients actually read our documents?

Peony is the strongest Box alternative for law firms that need to track how clients and opposing counsel interact with shared documents. Peony's page-level analytics (available on the Free plan) show which pages each recipient viewed, how long they spent on each page, and in what order — critical for knowing whether a client actually reviewed a retainer agreement or disclosure packet. Box only logs that a file was accessed, with no page-level detail. For law firms sharing sensitive case materials externally, Peony's purpose-built data rooms provide the engagement intelligence that Box lacks.

results.title: What is the best alternative to box? (Buxus sempervirens) | The RHS
results.content: With all the problems that we have   with box tree caterpillar and box tree blight, you may have reached a point where you just think I've had enough of fighting these waves, just like King Canute. And now is the time for change. We're here in the walled garden at RHS Garden Wisley today, and I'd like to show you some suggestions of box alternatives. Box is really, really hard to replace. It's a versatile plant. It grows in sun. It grows in shade. It grows in dry conditions. And of course, it's infinitely clippable. But there are things to look at. There are things that have done very, very well here. And I'd like to share with you some of the best alternatives. This is the Lonicera 'Maigrün'. and it's just freshly clipped, so it's looking very, very smart. It's a good one because it [...] you get really, really good cover down the sides when you clip it. So it clips rather nicely. It takes a little while to establish, but it really is a stalwart because like all yews, it tolerates dry conditions. It will tolerate shade. And here in the wall garden it's very, very sunny. And it doesn't mind that either. So as box alternatives go, it's a top notch plant. Just a quick word about Ilex crenata. Now, a good few years ago, when the problems with box blight and box tree caterpillar sort of emerged, Ilex crenata seemed to be the poster child as a box replacement, and it was widely planted. After a few years, we were finding in our advisory department the people were sending in samples of yellowing leaves and dieback, And it's really been found that there's quite a narrow range of [...] it's really been found that there's quite a narrow range of conditions that they like. They like a nice, moist summer, they like a bit of water in the summertime, and they don't like waterlogged conditions in the winter, So it does quite well in some areas of the country. But we found that to recommend it for the whole country at the same time, there are some reservations with that, so it's well worth bearing that in mind as you choose your box alternative. For a burst of colour in spring. Consider Rhododendron ‘Bloombux’. and it's covered in pink flowers, in the springtime. And it clips beautifully. Takes a little while to get established, to get to this lovely shape. This has been here a few years, really, really worth the wait. And the great thing about this one is it doesn't need acid

results.title: Box Alternatives for Healthy Hedges | RHS Advice
results.content:
Box (Buxus sempervirens) is one of the most versatile evergreen shrubs. It tolerates a wide range of soil conditions, light levels and temperatures. It is amenable to being trimmed and regenerates well when cut back into old wood. It only needs trimming once a year, and is cheap and easy to propagate.  
  
We asked professional gardeners, our advisors, RHS garden staff, the gardening public and members of the RHS which evergreen box alternatives they have been planting and how they have been performing. The results have helped inform our list of suggested alternatives. [...] ### Top tip

Teucrium is under-used as a box alternative. The flowers produced when the hedge is allowed to grow a little between clippings make it not only a very attractive option, but also a great choice for pollinators.

Ready to choose your box alternative? Head over to our best alternatives to box selection page to find a plant that’s right for your garden.

Corokia × virgata ‘Frosted Chocolate’

Cotoneaster conspicuus ‘Decorus’

Euonymus japonicus ‘Green Rocket’

Ilex crenata ‘Green Hedger’

Lonicera pileata

Myrtus communis ‘Merion’

Osmanthus × burkwoodii

Phillyrea angustifolia f. rosmarinifolia

Pittosporum ‘Arundel Green’

Podocarpus ‘County Park Fire’

Podocarpus nivalis

Rhododendron Bloombux

Sarcococca ruscifolia

Teucrium chamaedrys

Veronica ‘Nantyderry’

results.title: 10 Box.com Alternatives for Creative Businesses
results.content:
We’re reviewing Box.com and 9 popular alternatives including Dropbox, Soonr, Egnyte and Hightail. We’ll see whether these platforms can provide the tools creative businesses need when it comes to sharing and collaborating on files. serve the needs of businesses who generate creative materials.

From print to online, creative businesses have a lot of files to keep track of, and often they are collaborating with other agencies on a project. At any given time they might need to organize videos, images for print campaigns, website banners, and ux frameworks for mobile apps. When it’s time to work on a project for a well-know brand, creative agencies have an additional task of managing those brand assets, whether working in-house or on behalf of the brand. # Box.com Alternatives for Creative Businesses

## 10 Solutions Compared!

### Box.com is a well-known file sharing platform for businesses and there are a number of similar alternatives. Many articles on general business use cases of file sharing have been written that compare Box.com and its similar alternatives. [...] Surveying the Box.com competitors, we found three which offer online proofing – Huddle, Soonr and Filecamp. Huddle offers online proofing and review features that allow you to make notes on images and documents, receive emails regarding any changes, and send approval requests for final designs. Soonr has a simpler set of features that allow you to make notes, track signatures, and changes.
